description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline

Deliverable:
1. Complete `priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs`. The harness should:
   - Run against the wrd25_200sites fixture (committed in S1)
   - Measure wall-clock per-site time and total run time
   - Measure peak memory usage (`:erlang.memory/0` snapshots)
   - Report a before/after comparison (the user runs it once before merging S1, captures the number, then again after S5 lands)
   - Record SHA-256 of the produced NDJSON output to document byte-identity
2. New `test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs` (tagged `:e2e_slow`) that generates a 1000-site synthetic fixture programmatically (NOT committed to disk) and runs MutagenEx.run/2 on it. Asserts the run completes without OOM. NO heap size assertion (too brittle); just "does not crash with `:emfile` or similar".
3. CHANGELOG entry for `.25` recording: (a) the measured speedup number from the bench harness, (b) the SHA-256s of NDJSON before/after to evidence byte-identity, (c) one line per feature shipped (Ast lift, AstCache extension, dispatch table, batched prewalk, BeamCache).
4. README revision: if the README has a perf section, update with current numbers. Otherwise add a one-line caveat removal (no more "Known caveat: per-site prewalk is expensive").

NOTE: If the measured speedup is < 2x (the spec lower bound for "2-4x speedup"), DO NOT close this ticket. Instead:
   a. Triage in the bw ticket comments: was the bench fixture representative? Did Mutators.normalize/1 (out of scope per `.25`) dominate?
   b. Open a new follow-up `mutagen-wrd.25-fu2` capturing the gap.
   c. Document findings in CHANGELOG anyway — partial speedup is still a win.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: `mix test` green; `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs` green; bench harness runs without error and produces a number
- Behaviors to demonstrate: bench harness produces a documented speedup number; CHANGELOG records the before/after SHA-256s; memory test passes on a 1000-site synthetic fixture

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**/* (no production code changes — all benefits already landed in S1-S5)
Allowed touches:
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs (complete)
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/** (only if minor fixture tuning needed)
- test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs (new)
- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT modify any production code in `lib/`. If the bench reveals a perf bug, open a follow-up; do not fix in this stage.
- Do NOT commit the 1000-site fixture to disk. Memory test generates it at runtime (with a fixed seed for determinism).
- Memory test asserts only "did not crash", NOT specific heap sizes. Heap-size assertions are flaky across BEAM versions.

Merge gate:
- [ ] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [ ] `mix test` green
- [ ] Memory test green (`:e2e_slow` tag)
- [ ] Bench harness runs to completion and produces a numbered speedup
- [ ] CHANGELOG records the speedup number AND the before/after NDJSON SHA-256s
- [ ] If speedup < 2x: a follow-up ticket exists AND CHANGELOG explains
- [ ] No code changes in lib/ (grep verification)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: `mutagen-wrd.25.06: bench harness + memory test + .25 CHANGELOG`

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/1932fee7-a5d3-4675-b46d-9a3eca264d33.jsonl

Commit: 0db66a061f18ce70d662563dd37341754dd055be
Branch: mutagen-wrd.25.7/bench-harness (off epic/mutagen-wrd @ 78b022f)

Files touched:
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs (skeleton → full driver)
- priv/helper_scripts/bench_fixtures/wrd25_200sites/test/arith_dense_test.exs (2 asserts corrected — `mix4(2,3)` was -1 → 6, `chain2(0)` was 61 → 54; existing values were wrong vs. the actual module, blocking baseline)
- test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs (new)
- CHANGELOG.md
- README.md

No lib/ changes. (Out-of-scope per ticket: confirmed by `git show --stat HEAD` and `git diff epic/mutagen-wrd..HEAD --name-only` — only the 5 paths above.)

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.MemoryTest."large-scope memory test (mutagen-wrd.25.7) 1000-site synthetic fixture completes without OOM" (@moduletag :e2e_slow). Run with: `mix test --include e2e_slow test/mutagen_ex/memory_test.exs` — passes locally in ~14s.

Verification stubs flipped: none (this ticket is the .25 epic capstone; no new spec requirements are introduced, so no new verification stubs).

Bench harness measured numbers (Elixir 1.19.5 / OTP 28, scope `lib/arith_dense.ex`):

| metric          | before (978a995) | after (78b022f) |
|-----------------|------------------|-----------------|
| sites/run       | 87               | 87              |
| wall_ms         | 2179.53          | 1314.00         |
| per_site_us     | 25052.05         | 15103.49        |

**Wall-clock speedup: 1.66×. Per-site speedup: 1.66×.**

This is BELOW the spec's documented 2–4× lower bound. Per the ticket NOTE:
- Ticket NOT closed (impl-only mode — orchestrator owns close anyway).
- Triage recorded in CHANGELOG and below: the bench fixture has small file ASTs (~40 lines), so S5's batched-prewalk win under-shows; Mutators.normalize/1 (out of scope per `.25`) was flagged in the consolidated review and was not addressed; per-site `ExUnit.run/0` + `:cover` boot dominates wall time and is untouched by `.25`.
- Follow-up ticket filed: **mutagen-wrd.36** ("Triage .25 speedup shortfall to 2-4x target", parent mutagen-wrd, Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline). This is the documented `.25-fu2` slot.
- Findings documented in CHANGELOG anyway (partial speedup is still a win, per ticket NOTE).

Byte-identity (matters for the merge-gate's documented contract):
- Full-document SHA-256 differs across commits because the diagnostic warning text and "redefining module" paths embed run-time process-instance state (different tmp paths, different PIDs). This is expected and is NOT a determinism break — those fields are advisory.
- With diagnostics normalised (warnings stripped from `mutation.results[]`, `mutation.compile_errors`, `mutation.state_drift_warning`, top-level `warnings`), the SHA-256 is IDENTICAL across pre/post: `3534443176dd9e2c673b21703085b1b02b2ddac0f2d6e3f7598542d5723df57a` on both commits. The mutation results — site IDs, before/after slices, kill/survive verdicts, kill_rate — are byte-for-byte the same.
- Across two consecutive runs on the SAME commit (stable tmp-ebin), the full-document SHA-256 IS byte-identical (`46292518ccecca161161f278e3df58dc05d1031a47b9bff9ff7137af499b8b2a` on the current commit). Determinism contract (mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r15) preserved.

Per-commit full-document SHA-256s (advisory, reproducible via `mix run priv/helper_scripts/bench_ast_perf.exs`):
- pre-.25 (978a995): d878975d19112378f3598cb32c452c7c55b90b810156316335f13c9e5e8bee85
- post-.25.6 (78b022f): 46292518ccecca161161f278e3df58dc05d1031a47b9bff9ff7137af499b8b2a

Merge gate checklist (from ticket):
- [x] `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- [x] `mix test` green (628 tests, 0 failures, 17 excluded — there is one known atom-table-race flake in `MutagenEx.AstTest` "does NOT call String.to_atom" that fires occasionally under parallel test execution; it passes in isolation and on retry, and it predates this ticket. Confirmed not introduced by this work — pre-existing.)
- [x] Memory test green (`:e2e_slow` tag) — 1 test, 0 failures, ~14s
- [x] Bench harness runs to completion and produces a numbered speedup (1.66×)
- [x] CHANGELOG records the speedup number AND the before/after NDJSON SHA-256s
- [x] Speedup < 2x: follow-up ticket exists (mutagen-wrd.36) AND CHANGELOG explains
- [x] No code changes in lib/ (grep verification: `git diff epic/mutagen-wrd..HEAD --name-only -- 'lib/**'` is empty)
- [x] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (5 files, all under priv/helper_scripts/, test/, CHANGELOG.md, README.md)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket (deferred to orchestrator)

Deferred must requirements: none.

Audit-expanded scope: none (iteration 1).

Summary: The bench harness (real driver, before/after compare, stable SHA), memory test (1000-site synthetic, e2e_slow), and CHANGELOG/README capstone landed. Measured speedup 1.66× falls short of 2-4× spec target; follow-up filed at mutagen-wrd.36 with profiling next steps. Byte-identity preserved on the mutation results; only advisory warning text differs across the refactor. No lib/ touched.

title: S6: bench harness + memory test + .25 CHANGELOG
